name: io.github.hairglasses-studio.dotfiles-mcp
description: Publish mirror of the canonical dotfiles-mcp workstation MCP server with discovery-first tools, resources, and prompts.

tools.name: arch_aur_search
tools.description: Search AUR packages and return key metadata for the best matches.

tools.name: arch_file_owner
tools.description: Find the pacman package that owns a given file path.

tools.name: arch_mirror_status
tools.description: Return the healthiest official Arch Linux mirrors from the mirror-status JSON feed.

tools.name: arch_news_critical
tools.description: Return the latest Arch news items that look like manual-intervention or high-risk upgrade notices.

tools.name: arch_news_latest
tools.description: Return the latest Arch Linux news items from the official RSS feed.

tools.name: arch_orphans
tools.description: List orphaned packages from pacman -Qtdq.

tools.name: arch_package_info
tools.description: Fetch official Arch Linux package metadata for an exact package name.

tools.name: arch_package_search
tools.description: Search official Arch Linux packages by keyword.

tools.name: arch_pacman_log
tools.description: Read recent pacman log lines, optionally filtered by a substring.

tools.name: arch_pkgbuild_audit
tools.description: Audit an AUR PKGBUILD or raw PKGBUILD content for risky patterns.

tools.name: arch_updates_dry_run
tools.description: Show official and AUR updates without installing anything.

tools.name: archwiki_page
tools.description: Fetch an ArchWiki page with sections and cleaned page content.

tools.name: archwiki_search
tools.description: Search the ArchWiki and return matching pages with cleaned snippets.

tools.name: audio_device_switch
tools.description: Switch the default audio output device. Provide a PipeWire node ID (numeric) or a device name substring to match. Uses wpctl set-default.

tools.name: audio_devices
tools.description: List all audio devices (sinks and sources) with type, name, ID, and state. Uses pactl for structured output.

tools.name: audio_mute
tools.description: Toggle or set mute state for an audio sink. Actions: toggle, on (mute), off (unmute). Uses wpctl.

tools.name: audio_status
tools.description: Show current audio status: default sink/source, volume level, mute state. Uses wpctl to query PipeWire.

tools.name: audio_volume
tools.description: Set audio volume. Accepts absolute percentage (e.g. '50'), float 0-1 (e.g. '0.5'), or relative adjustment (e.g. '+5', '-10'). Uses wpctl to control PipeWire.

tools.name: bt_connect
tools.description: Connect to a Bluetooth device with BLE retry logic. Resolves names against all known devices (paired + scanned).

tools.name: bt_device_info
tools.description: Get detailed info for a Bluetooth device including battery, profiles, and trust status.

tools.name: bt_disconnect
tools.description: Disconnect a Bluetooth device.

tools.name: bt_discover_and_connect
tools.description: Full BLE-safe workflow: scan, find device by name, remove stale bond, pair with agent, trust, connect with retry. Handles BLE re-pairing (MAC changes, auth failures).

tools.name: bt_list_devices
tools.description: List Bluetooth devices with connection status and battery levels.

tools.name: bt_pair
tools.description: Pair with a Bluetooth device using interactive agent (handles BLE auth). Set remove_first=true to clear stale bonds before re-pairing.

tools.name: bt_power
tools.description: Toggle Bluetooth adapter power on or off.

tools.name: bt_remove
tools.description: Remove (forget) a paired Bluetooth device.

tools.name: bt_scan
tools.description: Scan for nearby Bluetooth devices. 'start' scans for 8 seconds (configurable) and returns discovered devices.

tools.name: bt_trust
tools.description: Trust or untrust a Bluetooth device.

tools.name: claude_crash_detect
tools.description: Detect Claude Code session crashes by checking PID liveness, dead session count within a time window, and crash indicators in history. Returns severity assessment (minor/major/catastrophic).

tools.name: claude_fleet_recovery
tools.description: Composed recovery workflow: detect dead sessions across all repos, analyze git state, generate recovery steps with resume commands. Dry-run by default — set execute=true to trigger resumes.

tools.name: claude_recovery_history
tools.description: Audit trail of crash and recovery events extracted from history.jsonl. Shows crash timestamps, resume attempts, and session outcomes.

tools.name: claude_recovery_report
tools.description: Comprehensive cross-session, cross-repo recovery report. Scans all sessions, correlates with git state, identifies orphaned repos, and builds a priority queue for session resumption ranked by incomplete tasks.

tools.name: claude_repo_diff
tools.description: Compare a repository against origin/main: unpushed commits, local-only branches, and stash entries.

tools.name: claude_repo_roadmap_status
tools.description: Parse ROADMAP.md checkbox items and compare completion against a session's task list. Returns progress percentage and unchecked items.

tools.name: claude_repo_status
tools.description: Get git status for a repository: current branch, last commit, staged/unstaged/untracked files, and remote tracking status.

tools.name: claude_session_compare
tools.description: Compare progress between two Claude Code sessions: task completion delta, commit delta, file change count, and time gap.

tools.name: claude_session_detail
tools.description: Deep inspection of a single Claude Code session: metadata, recent interaction logs, associated plan file, task list with statuses, and memory files.

tools.name: claude_session_health
tools.description: Health score (0-100) for a Claude Code session across 5 dimensions: process_alive, tasks_completion, git_clean, recency, plan_progress. Returns per-dimension scores and actionable recommendations.

tools.name: claude_session_logs
tools.description: Read the last N structured log entries from a Claude Code session's JSONL interaction log. Returns parsed entries with type, timestamp, and content summary.

tools.name: claude_session_replay
tools.description: Reconstruct the conversation thread from a session's JSONL log as user/assistant exchange pairs with tool use summaries.

tools.name: claude_session_scan
tools.description: Scan all Claude Code sessions, check PID liveness, and correlate with history for last activity timestamps. Returns per-session status (alive/dead), repo, name, and timestamps.

tools.name: claude_session_search
tools.description: Full-text keyword search across all Claude Code session JSONL logs. Finds sessions by topic, tool names, or content. Returns ranked matches with context snippets and resume commands.

tools.name: claude_session_tag
tools.description: Extract structured metadata tags for a Claude Code session: repo name, last commit timestamp, last session activity, plan file, and task counts.

tools.name: claude_workspace_snapshot
tools.description: Capture a point-in-time snapshot of all Claude Code sessions and repo git states across the studio directory. Saves to ~/.claude/snapshots/ for later comparison.

tools.name: clipboard_read
tools.description: Read the current Wayland clipboard contents as text. Uses wl-paste.

tools.name: clipboard_read_image
tools.description: Read an image from the Wayland clipboard. Uses wl-paste --type image/png. Returns the image inline as base64-encoded PNG.

tools.name: clipboard_write
tools.description: Write text to the Wayland clipboard. Uses wl-copy. Optionally specify a MIME type.

tools.name: desktop_act
tools.description: Invoke a specific AT-SPI action on a semantic element, such as activate, press, select, or grab focus.

tools.name: desktop_capabilities
tools.description: Check whether the embedded AT-SPI helper, python3, and pyatspi are available on this host.

tools.name: desktop_click
tools.description: Invoke the default clickable semantic action by ref, path, or by app plus name and optional role.

tools.name: desktop_click_text
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Find text on screen via OCR and click at its center. The atomic 'click the button labeled X' primitive. Requires explicit approval.

tools.name: desktop_find
tools.description: Find a semantic element by app plus name, role, states, ref, or a previously returned path.

tools.name: desktop_find_all
tools.description: Find all semantic elements matching an app plus name, role, states, ref, or path.

tools.name: desktop_find_text
tools.description: Screenshot + OCR + locate text on screen. Returns bounding box coordinates in desktop space suitable for ydotool. Use to find where a button or label appears before clicking.

tools.name: desktop_focus
tools.description: Focus a semantic element by ref, path, or by app plus name and optional role, using AT-SPI component focus or a focus action fallback.

tools.name: desktop_focus_window
tools.description: Focus a top-level semantic desktop window by title, app/class substring, or a previously returned semantic ref/path.

tools.name: desktop_key
tools.description: Send raw ydotool key events after a semantic target has focus.

tools.name: desktop_list_windows
tools.description: List semantic desktop windows with their app identity, refs, bounds, and current value metadata.

tools.name: desktop_project_open
tools.description: Open a project scene by combining an optional monitor preset, optional Hyprland layout, and an optional tmux bootstrap in the target repo path.

tools.name: desktop_read_value
tools.description: Read the current text or numeric value from a semantic element, such as an entry, label, slider, or spinbox.

tools.name: desktop_screenshot_ocr
tools.description: Screenshot the full desktop or a specific window (by address/class) and extract text via OCR. Returns both the image (for LLM vision) and extracted text. Use for seeing what is on screen.

tools.name: desktop_set_text
tools.description: Set the text contents of an editable semantic element. Pass an empty string to clear the field.

tools.name: desktop_set_value
tools.description: Set the numeric value of a semantic element such as a slider, dial, or spinbox.

tools.name: desktop_snapshot
tools.description: List running AT-SPI applications and high-level semantic metadata before targeting a window.

tools.name: desktop_target_windows
tools.description: Read the semantic accessibility tree for one application, bounded by depth.

tools.name: desktop_type
tools.description: Type text as a semantic desktop follow-up after a semantic target has focus.

tools.name: desktop_wait_for_element
tools.description: Wait for a semantic element to appear or satisfy the requested states, then return its resolved element record.

tools.name: desktop_wait_for_text
tools.description: Poll screenshot+OCR until text appears on screen or timeout. Returns coordinates and final screenshot when found. Use to wait for UI state changes (dialogs, loading, notifications).

tools.name: dotfiles_bulk_pipeline
tools.description: Run build+test pipeline across multiple repos. Detects language and runs appropriate build/test steps. Returns per-repo pass/fail with output.

tools.name: dotfiles_cascade_reload
tools.description: Atomic multi-service reload with health verification. Reloads services in order (default: hyprland → mako → eww), verifying each is healthy before proceeding to the next.

tools.name: dotfiles_changelog_gen
tools.description: Generate changelog previews across selected workspace repos by reusing the ops changelog engine. Optionally write CHANGELOG.md updates in place.

tools.name: dotfiles_check_symlinks
tools.description: Check health of all expected dotfiles symlinks (healthy, broken, or missing).

tools.name: dotfiles_create_repo
tools.description: Scaffold a new hairglasses-studio repo with standard files (CI, LICENSE, .editorconfig, pre-commit hooks). Auto-detects or uses specified language for templates.

tools.name: dotfiles_dep_audit
tools.description: Audit Go dependency version skew across all hairglasses-studio repos. Reports which deps are unified vs which have version drift.

tools.name: dotfiles_desktop_status
tools.description: Report desktop control readiness, including Hyprland, AT-SPI semantic targeting, session helpers, screenshot/OCR, input injection, and the kitty-to-ghostty terminal shader pipeline.

tools.name: dotfiles_eww_get
tools.description: Get the current value of an eww variable. Useful for debugging bar widgets (e.g. bar_workspaces_dp1, bar_cpu, bar_shader, rg_fleet).

tools.name: dotfiles_eww_inspect
tools.description: Inspect the current eww daemon state, active and defined windows, visible layer bindings, and parsed variable map.

tools.name: dotfiles_eww_reload
tools.description: Reload eww narrowly when possible by reopening one target window, falling back to full eww reload and then daemon restart only when needed.

tools.name: dotfiles_eww_restart
tools.description: Kill all eww and waybar processes, restart eww daemon, and open the primary eww surface (sidebar). Use after editing eww config files.

tools.name: dotfiles_eww_status
tools.description: Show eww bar status: daemon health, open windows, layer surfaces, key variable values, and whether waybar is incorrectly running.

tools.name: dotfiles_fleet_audit
tools.description: Comprehensive fleet audit: per-repo language, Go version, remote CI, cached local baseline status, workflow-governance state, test count, last commit age, pipeline.mk and CLAUDE.md presence.

tools.name: dotfiles_fleet_baseline_refresh
tools.description: Run each repo's local baseline on the current main checkout, cache the results under docs/agent-parity, and return the refreshed local-baseline snapshot set.

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_bulk_archive
tools.description: Archive multiple repos in a GitHub org. Useful for housekeeping old reference repos. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_bulk_clone
tools.description: Clone all missing org repos to the local directory. Skips repos already cloned. Pairs with dotfiles_gh_local_sync_audit to act on missing repos. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_bulk_settings
tools.description: Batch-apply repo settings across multiple org repos. Supports auto-delete head branches, wiki, projects, and merge strategy toggles. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_clean_stale
tools.description: Remove local clones that have no matching repo in the GitHub org. Identifies orphaned directories (local git repos whose origin doesn't point to the org). Set execute=true to delete (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_full_sync
tools.description: One-command fleet sync: pulls all local repos, identifies missing org repos, clones them, and reports orphaned dirs. Set execute=true to clone missing repos (dry-run by default for clones; pull always runs).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_list_org_repos
tools.description: List all repos in a GitHub org with local clone sync status. Shows whether each repo is cloned locally, missing, or has a wrong remote.

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_list_personal_repos
tools.description: List GitHub repos under a personal account, optionally excluding repos already in an org. Shows fork status, visibility, and language.

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_local_sync_audit
tools.description: Audit local directory against GitHub org repos. Finds orphaned local dirs (no org repo), missing clones (org repo not cloned), and remote mismatches.

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_onboard_repos
tools.description: Onboard public repos into a GitHub org for architecture reference. For each repo: forks to org (if not already there), clones locally, strips git history to a single squashed commit on the default branch, and force-pushes. Designed for batch operations on 8-15+ repos. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_pull_all
tools.description: Fetch or pull updates for all git repos in the local directory. Uses --ff-only by default to avoid merge conflicts. Set fetch_only=true to just fetch without merging.

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_recreate_forks
tools.description: Recreate forked repos as fresh repos under a GitHub org. Clones each fork, squashes all history to a single initial commit on the default branch, creates a new repo in the org, pushes, and deletes the original fork. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_gh_transfer_repos
tools.description: Bulk transfer non-fork repos from a personal account to a GitHub org. Skips repos that already exist in the target. Set execute=true to run (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_go_sync
tools.description: Sync Go version across all repos to match dotfiles/make/go-version. Updates go.mod and optionally runs go mod tidy.

tools.name: dotfiles_health_check
tools.description: Run org-wide health dashboard across all hairglasses-studio repos. Shows build status, Go version, pipeline.mk inclusion, and CI workflow presence for every repo.

tools.name: dotfiles_list_configs
tools.description: List all dotfiles config directories with symlink health and detected format.

tools.name: dotfiles_mcpkit_version_sync
tools.description: Sync mcpkit dependency version across all thin MCP servers. Finds latest mcpkit version and updates go.mod in each MCP repo. Set execute=true to run go get + go mod tidy (dry-run by default).

tools.name: dotfiles_onboard_repo
tools.description: Onboard a repo with hairglasses-studio standard files (.editorconfig, CI workflows, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, pre-commit hooks). Detects language and adds appropriate config.

tools.name: dotfiles_oss_check
tools.description: Run open-source readiness checks for a single category (community, readme, gomod, testing, cicd, security, release, maintenance)

tools.name: dotfiles_oss_score
tools.description: Score a repository's open-source readiness (0-100) across 8 categories based on GitHub best practices, OpenSSF Scorecard criteria, and Go module conventions

tools.name: dotfiles_pipeline_run
tools.description: Run the build+test pipeline (hg-pipeline.sh) on a repo. Supports Go, Node.js, and Python. Returns JSON results with per-step timing.

tools.name: dotfiles_pipeline_status
tools.description: Aggregate remote CI and cached local baseline signals across repos in one view. Optionally refresh baseline data first and filter to specific repos.

tools.name: dotfiles_release
tools.description: Run dry-run or execute release orchestration across explicit repo/version targets by reusing the ops release engine.

tools.name: dotfiles_reload_service
tools.description: Reload a desktop service after config changes.

tools.name: dotfiles_rice_check
tools.description: Rice health check: reports compositor, shader, wallpaper, service states, and (with level=full) scans all configs for non-Snazzy palette violations.

tools.name: dotfiles_server_health
tools.description: Show the active dotfiles-mcp contract shape: discovery coverage, tool counts, profile, and resource/prompt availability.

tools.name: dotfiles_tool_catalog
tools.description: Summarize the dotfiles tool catalog by category with deferred-loading hints.

tools.name: dotfiles_tool_schema
tools.description: Inspect one tool descriptor including schemas, search terms, and deferred-loading hints.

tools.name: dotfiles_tool_search
tools.description: Search the dotfiles tool catalog by keyword before invoking a desktop or workflow tool.

tools.name: dotfiles_tool_stats
tools.description: Show high-level catalog statistics, including how many tools are marked deferred in the active profile.

tools.name: dotfiles_validate_config
tools.description: Validate config file content syntax (TOML or JSON).

tools.name: dotfiles_workflow_sync
tools.description: Sync CI workflow files across all repos from canonical sources. Detects stale workflows and optionally updates, commits, and pushes.

tools.name: gh_star_lists_delete
tools.description: Delete GitHub star folders (user lists) by name. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_star_lists_ensure
tools.description: Create or update GitHub star folders (user lists). Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_star_lists_list
tools.description: List the current GitHub star folders (user lists), optionally including list items.

tools.name: gh_star_lists_rename
tools.description: Rename an existing GitHub star folder while preserving its description and privacy settings. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_star_membership_set
tools.description: Update which GitHub star folders a repo belongs to. Supports merge, replace, remove, and replace_managed. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_stars_audit_markdown
tools.description: Audit markdown-derived GitHub repo lists against the matching live GitHub star folders without mutating anything.

tools.name: gh_stars_bootstrap
tools.description: Bootstrap the GitHub Stars workflow: star the researched MCP repos, create the MCP / Production|Experimental|Alpha folders, assign repos, and optionally install the Codex MCP entries. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_stars_cleanup_candidates
tools.description: Find starred repositories that look like cleanup candidates, such as archived repos, forks, stale repos, unlisted repos, or repos missing a managed prefix.

tools.name: gh_stars_install_codex_mcp
tools.description: Install the global Codex MCP entries for the official GitHub server and the dotfiles GitHub Stars workflow surface. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_stars_list
tools.description: List starred GitHub repositories with optional filters and current GitHub star-list membership.

tools.name: gh_stars_set
tools.description: Star or unstar GitHub repositories in batch. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_stars_summary
tools.description: Summarize current GitHub stars with counts by list, language, topic, archived/fork totals, and optional managed-prefix coverage.

tools.name: gh_stars_sync_markdown
tools.description: Parse markdown GitHub links into per-file star lists, then exactly reconcile those target lists while preserving unrelated list memberships. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gh_stars_taxonomy_audit
tools.description: Audit managed GitHub star-list coverage and compare current stars against an optional desired taxonomy profile.

tools.name: gh_stars_taxonomy_suggest
tools.description: Suggest useful GitHub star folders from your current stars using GitHub suggestions plus topic, language, and keyword clustering.

tools.name: gh_stars_taxonomy_sync
tools.description: Ensure GitHub star folders and reconcile repo membership from an explicit repo-to-list taxonomy. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: gpu_status
tools.description: Query NVIDIA GPU status: driver, temperature, utilization, memory, power draw, and running GPU processes.

tools.name: hypr_capture_events
tools.description: Capture one or more Hyprland IPC events from .socket2.sock.

tools.name: hypr_click
tools.description: Click at absolute screen coordinates using ydotool.

tools.name: hypr_close_window
tools.description: Close a window. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_dismiss_notify
tools.description: Dismiss the most recent Hyprland notification via hyprctl dismissnotify.

tools.name: hypr_dispatch
tools.description: Run an arbitrary Hyprland dispatcher via hyprctl dispatch.

tools.name: hypr_focus_window
tools.description: Focus a window by its address or class name.

tools.name: hypr_fullscreen_window
tools.description: Toggle fullscreen for a window. Focuses the window first, then applies fullscreen. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_get_active_window
tools.description: Return the active Hyprland window as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_get_active_workspace
tools.description: Return the active Hyprland workspace as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_get_config_errors
tools.description: Return Hyprland config parse errors and whether the active config is healthy.

tools.name: hypr_get_cursor_position
tools.description: Return the current cursor position as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_get_monitors
tools.description: List connected monitors with resolution, refresh rate, position, scale, and active workspace.

tools.name: hypr_get_option
tools.description: Read a Hyprland option value via hyprctl getoption.

tools.name: hypr_get_prop
tools.description: Read a Hyprland window property via hyprctl getprop.

tools.name: hypr_get_system_info
tools.description: Return Hyprland system information from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_get_version
tools.description: Return Hyprland version data as raw JSON or text from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_hyprpaper
tools.description: Run a Hyprland hyprpaper subcommand through hyprctl hyprpaper.

tools.name: hypr_hyprsunset
tools.description: Run a Hyprland hyprsunset subcommand through hyprctl hyprsunset.

tools.name: hypr_key
tools.description: Send key events using ydotool. Use Linux input event codes (e.g. '29:1 29:0' for Ctrl tap, '56:1 31:1 31:0 56:0' for Alt+S).

tools.name: hypr_layout_list
tools.description: List saved Hyprland layout snapshots from local state.

tools.name: hypr_layout_restore
tools.description: Best-effort restore of a saved Hyprland layout. Reuses matching windows first and only launches missing apps when the saved scene has explicit launch commands.

tools.name: hypr_layout_save
tools.description: Save the current Hyprland window/workspace scene with class, title, workspace, monitor, floating state, size, position, fullscreen, pinning, and optional launch commands.

tools.name: hypr_list_binds
tools.description: Return the configured Hyprland keybinds as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_list_devices
tools.description: Return the connected Hyprland devices as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_list_layers
tools.description: Return the Hyprland layer-shell surfaces as raw JSON from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_list_layouts
tools.description: Return the available Hyprland layouts as raw JSON or text from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_list_windows
tools.description: List all windows managed by Hyprland with their address, title, class, workspace, size, position, and focus state.

tools.name: hypr_list_workspace_rules
tools.description: Return Hyprland workspace rules as raw JSON or text from hyprctl.

tools.name: hypr_list_workspaces
tools.description: List all Hyprland workspaces with window count, monitor, and focused status.

tools.name: hypr_minimize_window
tools.description: Minimize a window by moving it to the special:minimized workspace. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_monitor_preset_list
tools.description: List saved Hyprland monitor presets from local state.

tools.name: hypr_monitor_preset_restore
tools.description: Restore a saved Hyprland monitor preset. Validates the generated hyprctl commands first, then applies them unless dry_run=true.

tools.name: hypr_monitor_preset_save
tools.description: Save the current Hyprland monitor arrangement into a local preset, including resolution, refresh, position, scale, transform, and enabled state.

tools.name: hypr_move_window
tools.description: Move a window to exact pixel coordinates. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_notify
tools.description: Show a Hyprland notification using hyprctl notify.

tools.name: hypr_output
tools.description: Run a Hyprland output subcommand such as create remove list or dpms through hyprctl output.

tools.name: hypr_plugin
tools.description: Run a Hyprland plugin subcommand through hyprctl plugin.

tools.name: hypr_reload_config
tools.description: Reload the Hyprland configuration and check for config errors.

tools.name: hypr_resize_window
tools.description: Resize a window to exact pixel dimensions. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_rolling_log
tools.description: Return a recent snapshot of the Hyprland rolling log.

tools.name: hypr_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot. Specify a monitor name for a single output, or omit to capture all monitors combined. For per-monitor detail (especially menubars), use hypr_screenshot_monitors instead.

tools.name: hypr_screenshot_monitors
tools.description: Capture separate screenshots of each monitor. Returns multiple images, one per active monitor, each resized individually for better detail than a single combined capture. Also captures a cropped menubar view (top 48px) of each monitor for bar inspection.

tools.name: hypr_screenshot_window
tools.description: Take a screenshot of a specific window by address or class name. Returns the image inline for LLM vision.

tools.name: hypr_set_cursor
tools.description: Set the active Hyprland cursor theme and size.

tools.name: hypr_set_keyword
tools.description: Set a Hyprland keyword via hyprctl keyword.

tools.name: hypr_set_monitor
tools.description: Configure a monitor's resolution, position, or scale.

tools.name: hypr_set_prop
tools.description: Set a Hyprland window property via hyprctl setprop.

tools.name: hypr_switch_workspace
tools.description: Switch to a workspace by ID.

tools.name: hypr_switch_xkb_layout
tools.description: Switch a Hyprland keyboard layout via hyprctl switchxkblayout.

tools.name: hypr_toggle_floating
tools.description: Toggle floating state of a window. Resolves window by address or class.

tools.name: hypr_type_text
tools.description: Type text at the current cursor position using wtype (Wayland).

tools.name: hypr_wait_for_event
tools.description: Wait for a specific Hyprland IPC event on .socket2.sock.

tools.name: input_auto_setup_controller
tools.description: Detect all connected controllers, generate makima profiles for each, and optionally restart makima. Single tool replaces detect + generate + restart.

tools.name: input_controller_test
tools.description: Read events from a controller for 3 seconds to verify it works. Returns last 10 button/axis events.

tools.name: input_delete_makima_profile
tools.description: Delete a makima per-app profile.

tools.name: input_detect_controllers
tools.description: Scan for connected gamepad/controller devices. Returns brand (xbox/playstation/nintendo), capabilities, event path, and whether a makima profile exists.

tools.name: input_generate_controller_profile
tools.description: Generate a starter makima TOML profile for a controller with preset mappings (desktop, claude-code, gaming, media). Includes brand-aware button label comments.

tools.name: input_get_juhradial_battery
tools.description: Read MX battery status from the juhradial D-Bus daemon, falling back to bluetoothctl when D-Bus is unavailable.

tools.name: input_get_juhradial_config
tools.description: Read the tracked juhradial config.json from dotfiles.

tools.name: input_get_juhradial_profiles
tools.description: Read the tracked juhradial profiles.json from dotfiles.

tools.name: input_get_makima_profile
tools.description: Read a specific makima per-app profile by name.

tools.name: input_key_press
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Press a key combination (e.g. ctrl+shift+t, Return, super) using wtype -k or ydotool key. Simulates real key events — requires explicit approval.

tools.name: input_list_makima_profiles
tools.description: List all makima per-app button remapping profiles with their remap and command bindings.

tools.name: input_mouse_click
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Click mouse button at current or specified position using ydotool. Simulates a real mouse click — requires explicit approval.

tools.name: input_mouse_move
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Move mouse cursor to coordinates using ydotool mousemove. Simulates real mouse movement — requires explicit approval.

tools.name: input_mouse_scroll
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Scroll the mouse wheel up or down using ydotool. Simulates real scroll events — requires explicit approval.

tools.name: input_restart_services
tools.description: Restart juhradial mouse services or the makima controller service. Restarting the controller service may require elevated systemd permissions.

tools.name: input_screenshot_click
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Take a screenshot, then click at the specified coordinates. Combines screenshot capture for agent visual context with coordinate-based clicking. Requires explicit approval.

tools.name: input_set_juhradial_config
tools.description: Write dotfiles juhradial config.json after validating that the content is valid JSON.

tools.name: input_set_juhradial_profiles
tools.description: Write dotfiles juhradial profiles.json after validating that the content is valid JSON.

tools.name: input_set_makima_profile
tools.description: Create or update a makima per-app profile. Validates TOML before writing.

tools.name: input_status
tools.description: Show running state of juhradial mouse services, the makima controller service, and MX battery status when available.

tools.name: input_type_text
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Type text as if from keyboard using wtype (Wayland) or ydotool as fallback. This simulates real keystrokes — requires explicit approval.

tools.name: investigate_port
tools.description: Investigate a TCP port: find the listening process, show its tree, check systemd unit status, and fetch recent logs. Single tool replaces port_list + ps_list + systemd_status + systemd_logs.

tools.name: investigate_service
tools.description: Investigate a systemd service: get status, find its processes, check its ports, and fetch recent logs. Single tool replaces systemd_status + ps_list + port_list + systemd_logs.

tools.name: kill_process
tools.description: Send a signal to a process. Supports TERM, KILL, HUP, INT, USR1, USR2, STOP, CONT.

tools.name: kitty_close_tab
tools.description: Close the active or matched kitty tabs.

tools.name: kitty_close_window
tools.description: Close the active or matched kitty windows.

tools.name: kitty_focus_tab
tools.description: Focus the active window in a matched kitty tab.

tools.name: kitty_focus_window
tools.description: Focus the active or matched kitty window.

tools.name: kitty_get_text
tools.description: Read plain or ANSI-formatted text from the active or matched kitty window.

tools.name: kitty_last_used_layout
tools.description: Restore the previous kitty layout for the active or matched tabs.

tools.name: kitty_launch
tools.description: Launch a new kitty window, tab, overlay, background task, or OS window.

tools.name: kitty_list_tabs
tools.description: List kitty tabs with OS window, title, layout, focus, and window count.

tools.name: kitty_list_windows
tools.description: List kitty windows with IDs, titles, cwd, pid, focus state, and command lines.

tools.name: kitty_load_config
tools.description: Reload one or more kitty config files, optionally with transient overrides.

tools.name: kitty_resize_os_window
tools.description: Resize or toggle state for a kitty OS window.

tools.name: kitty_resize_window
tools.description: Resize a kitty pane within the current layout.

tools.name: kitty_run_remote
tools.description: Run an arbitrary kitty remote-control subcommand when the dedicated tools do not cover the exact action.

tools.name: kitty_send_key
tools.description: Send key chords directly to the active or matched kitty windows.

tools.name: kitty_send_text
tools.description: Send arbitrary text to the active or matched kitty windows.

tools.name: kitty_set_font_size
tools.description: Set the kitty font size in points or with a relative delta.

tools.name: kitty_set_layout
tools.description: Set the kitty tab layout for the active or matched tabs.

tools.name: kitty_set_opacity
tools.description: Set the kitty background opacity for the active, matched, or all windows.

tools.name: kitty_set_tab_title
tools.description: Set or reset the title of the active or matched kitty tabs.

tools.name: kitty_set_theme
tools.description: Apply a kitty color theme from DOTFILES_DIR/kitty or by dumping a named kitten theme.

tools.name: kitty_set_title
tools.description: Set or reset the title of the active or matched kitty windows.

tools.name: kitty_show_image
tools.description: Show an image in an overlay kitty window using kitten icat.

tools.name: kitty_status
tools.description: Show kitty remote-control readiness, active connection status, and local theme candidates.

tools.name: mapping_auto_setup
tools.description: Detect all connected controllers and MIDI devices, generate missing profiles from templates, and optionally restart makima. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: mapping_daemon_control
tools.description: Control the mapitall daemon. Actions: status (health/uptime), reload (hot-reload profiles), list_devices (connected controllers), list_profiles (loaded profiles), get_state (variables/active app), set_variable (set a runtime variable).

tools.name: mapping_delete_profile
tools.description: Delete a mapping profile from disk.

tools.name: mapping_diff
tools.description: Compare two mapping profiles and show differences in their bindings.

tools.name: mapping_export
tools.description: Export a mapping profile for sharing. Optionally sanitize to strip local paths.

tools.name: mapping_generate
tools.description: Generate a mapping profile from a built-in template for a specified device.

tools.name: mapping_get_profile
tools.description: Read a mapping profile with parsed structure and raw TOML content.

tools.name: mapping_import
tools.description: Import a mapping profile from TOML content. Validates before writing. Dry-run mode available.

tools.name: mapping_learn
tools.description: Listen to a controller or MIDI device and capture which controls are moved. Use this to discover available inputs before creating mappings. Returns detected controls with type classification (button, axis, encoder, MIDI CC/note) and mapping suggestions.

tools.name: mapping_list_profiles
tools.description: List all mapping profiles with device info, format, and mapping counts. Supports filtering by format (unified/legacy), device name, and tag.

tools.name: mapping_list_templates
tools.description: List all available mapping templates for gamepad and MIDI controllers.

tools.name: mapping_migrate_legacy
tools.description: Convert legacy makima profiles to the unified mapping format. Dry-run by default.

tools.name: mapping_monitor
tools.description: Live-monitor controller events for debugging. Shows raw events with timestamps. Use to verify a controller is working and identify input codes.

tools.name: mapping_quick_map
tools.description: Add a single mapping rule to an existing profile without writing full TOML. Specify the input source, output type, and target. Creates the profile if it doesn't exist.

tools.name: mapping_resolve_test
tools.description: Simulate rule resolution: given an input source and app context, show which mapping rule would fire.

tools.name: mapping_set_profile
tools.description: Create or update a mapping profile. Validates TOML syntax and schema before writing.

tools.name: mapping_status
tools.description: Show complete controller mapping status: connected devices, active profiles, template availability, and directory paths. Call this first to understand the current setup.

tools.name: mapping_validate
tools.description: Validate a mapping profile for schema compliance. Provide either content (TOML string) or name (profile on disk).

tools.name: midi_generate_mapping
tools.description: Generate a MIDI controller mapping config (TOML) with preset templates: desktop-control (volume/brightness/workspaces), shader-control (shader/wallpaper cycling), volume-mixer (per-sink audio).

tools.name: midi_get_mapping
tools.description: Read an existing MIDI controller mapping config from dotfiles.

tools.name: midi_list_devices
tools.description: Detect connected USB MIDI controllers via ALSA. Returns card/device numbers, names, raw MIDI paths, and whether a mapping config exists in dotfiles.

tools.name: midi_set_mapping
tools.description: Create or update a MIDI controller mapping config. Validates TOML before writing.

tools.name: network_connections
tools.description: List all saved NetworkManager connection profiles (WiFi, VPN, Ethernet, etc.) with name, UUID, type, and device.

tools.name: network_dns
tools.description: Show DNS servers per network interface. Tries resolvectl first, falls back to /etc/resolv.conf.

tools.name: network_status
tools.description: Show overall network status: connectivity state, WiFi enabled, and active connections. Uses nmcli.

tools.name: network_vpn_toggle
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Connect or disconnect a VPN/connection profile by name. Use action 'up' to connect and 'down' to disconnect. Requires explicit approval.

tools.name: network_wifi_connect
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Connect to a WiFi network by SSID. Provide password for protected networks, omit for open networks. Requires explicit approval.

tools.name: network_wifi_list
tools.description: Scan and list available WiFi networks sorted by signal strength. Returns SSID, signal, security, channel, and frequency. Deduplicates SSIDs keeping strongest signal.

tools.name: notify_count
tools.description: Get the current notification count from SwayNotificationCenter.

tools.name: notify_dismiss
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Dismiss notifications in SwayNotificationCenter. Scope: all (dismiss everything) or latest (dismiss most recent only).

tools.name: notify_dnd
tools.description: Manage Do Not Disturb mode in SwayNotificationCenter. Actions: get (query current state), toggle, on, off.

tools.name: notify_history
tools.description: Get SwayNotificationCenter status snapshot: notification count, DND state, panel visibility, inhibitor state. (swaync does not expose individual notification history via CLI.)

tools.name: notify_history_clear
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Clear the locally logged notification history. By default entries are marked dismissed; set purge=true to remove them entirely.

tools.name: notify_history_entries
tools.description: Get the locally logged notification history entries with app, summary, body, urgency, timestamp, and dismissal state.

tools.name: notify_panel
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: Control the SwayNotificationCenter panel visibility. Actions: toggle, open, close.

tools.name: notify_send
tools.description: Send a desktop notification via notify-send. Supports urgency levels, timeout, and custom icons.

tools.name: ops_analyze_failures
tools.description: Categorize build/test failures into types (compile_error, type_error, missing_dep, test_assertion, timeout, import_cycle) with suggested fix order. Pass build_errors from ops_build and/or test_failures from ops_test_smart.

tools.name: ops_auto_fix
tools.description: Auto-fix mechanical failures from ops_analyze_failures: missing deps (go mod tidy), missing imports (goimports), unused vars (remove). Pass issues from analyze output. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true to apply patches.

tools.name: ops_branch_create
tools.description: Create a feature branch with conventional naming (feat/fix/chore prefix). Dry-run by default — pass execute=true to create.

tools.name: ops_build
tools.description: Run go build and parse compiler errors into structured JSON with file, line, column, message, and package. Returns success/failure with error count.

tools.name: ops_changed_files
tools.description: List changed files with diff stats and Go package mapping. Useful for understanding what was modified before building or testing.

tools.name: ops_changelog_generate
tools.description: Generate markdown changelog from conventional commits since last tag. Groups by feat/fix/chore/etc. Pass write=true to prepend to CHANGELOG.md.

tools.name: ops_ci_status
tools.description: Check GitHub Actions check status for a branch or PR. Returns per-check pass/fail with overall verdict. Use wait=true to poll until all checks complete (up to 5 minutes).

tools.name: ops_commit
tools.description: Stage changed files and create a conventional commit. Validates message format, never amends. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true to commit.

tools.name: ops_coverage_report
tools.description: Run test coverage and return per-package percentages. Set threshold>0 to gate on minimum overall coverage. Go: go test -coverprofile, Node: nyc/c8, Python: coverage.py.

tools.name: ops_dep_graph
tools.description: Generate a dependency graph for a Go workspace or module. Uses go mod graph for accurate module-level dependencies. Outputs Mermaid markdown (for GitHub rendering) or DOT format. Filter to internal org modules or include all transitive deps.

tools.name: ops_fleet_diff
tools.description: Show what changed across all repos since a date or ref. Returns per-repo commit counts, insertions/deletions, commit type breakdown (feat/fix/chore), and fleet-wide totals. Use since='3d' for relative or '2024-04-01' for absolute.

tools.name: ops_fleet_iterate
tools.description: Run build+test across all repos in a directory. Returns per-repo health matrix with build/test status, error counts, and overall fleet summary. Scans for Go/Node/Python projects.

tools.name: ops_iterate
tools.description: Core SDLC loop tool: build → test (changed packages) → analyze failures → track iteration. Call this after each round of edits. Returns structured next_actions with files to fix. Tracks iteration count and error trends for convergence detection.

tools.name: ops_iteration_patterns
tools.description: Analyze historical SDLC sessions for patterns: common failure types, average iterations to convergence, hot files that appear in most failures. Useful for identifying systemic issues and improving auto-fix heuristics.

tools.name: ops_lint_fix
tools.description: Auto-fix lint issues in-place. Go: goimports + golangci-lint --fix, Node: eslint --fix + prettier, Python: black + isort. Reports files changed and remaining errors.

tools.name: ops_pr_create
tools.description: Push branch and create a GitHub PR via gh CLI. Returns PR URL and number. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true to create.

tools.name: ops_pre_push
tools.description: Pre-push gate: run vet → lint → build → test (changed packages only). Short-circuits on first failure. Returns structured per-step results.

tools.name: ops_release
tools.description: Bump version, generate changelog entry, commit, and tag. Detects language (Go/Node/Python) and updates the appropriate version file. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true.

tools.name: ops_repo_analyze
tools.description: Analyze a repo and tag it with languages, protocols (gRPC, REST, MCP, WebSocket), frameworks, key dependencies, and project metadata (CI, tests, MCP server detection). Returns structured repo profile.

tools.name: ops_research_check
tools.description: Search the docs knowledge base for existing research on a topic. Returns matching documents with relevance scores and identifies gaps where no research exists. Check this before starting new research to avoid duplication.

tools.name: ops_revert
tools.description: Safely undo the last commit. If unpushed: soft reset (keeps changes staged). If pushed: creates a revert commit. Never force-pushes. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true.

tools.name: ops_session_create
tools.description: Create a new SDLC session for iteration tracking. Optional — ops_iterate auto-creates one if absent.

tools.name: ops_session_handoff
tools.description: Generate an Agent Handoff Protocol document from the current ops session and git state. Captures branch, dirty files, iteration history, and pending work. Set write=true to persist to repo.

tools.name: ops_session_list
tools.description: List all active SDLC sessions with summary stats. Auto-cleans sessions older than 7 days.

tools.name: ops_session_status
tools.description: Get SDLC session status: iteration count, error trend, convergence detection, total time spent.

tools.name: ops_ship
tools.description: Ship changes: pre-push gate → commit → push → create PR. Only proceeds if gate passes. Dry-run by default — pass execute=true to ship.

tools.name: ops_tech_debt
tools.description: Score tech debt 0-100 across 6 dimensions: dependency freshness, test coverage, lint cleanliness, CI health, documentation, code age. Single repo or fleet mode. Set store=true for trend tracking.

tools.name: ops_test_smart
tools.description: Run go test -json on only changed packages (via git diff), returning per-test pass/fail/skip results. Use all=true to test everything. Smart filtering avoids running the full suite on every iteration.

tools.name: port_list
tools.description: List listening TCP ports with process info via ss. Optionally filter by port number.

tools.name: prompt_capture
tools.description: Capture a prompt with auto-scoring. Computes SHA-256 hash for dedup, classifies task type, scores against 10 quality dimensions, and saves to docs/prompts/{repo}/unsorted/. Use when you want to store a prompt for later retrieval and analysis.

tools.name: prompt_export
tools.description: Export prompts matching filters as JSONL or markdown. Use for batch analysis, training data export, or prompt collection sharing.

tools.name: prompt_get
tools.description: Retrieve a single prompt by full hash or short hash (first 12 chars). Returns the complete prompt text and all metadata. Use when you need the full content of a specific prompt.

tools.name: prompt_improve
tools.description: Improve a prompt via the 13-stage enhancement pipeline (specificity, positive reframing, tone, XML structure, context reordering, format enforcement, etc). Saves the improved version to sorted/. Works on stored prompts (by hash) or raw text.

tools.name: prompt_score
tools.description: Score a prompt against 10 quality dimensions (Clarity, Specificity, Context, Structure, Examples, DocPlacement, Role, TaskFocus, Format, Tone). Returns 0-100 overall score with per-dimension breakdown. Works on stored prompts (by hash) or raw text.

tools.name: prompt_search
tools.description: Search the prompt registry with full-text query and/or tag/repo/status/score filters. Returns previews sorted by recency. Use when finding previously captured prompts or exploring prompt patterns.

tools.name: prompt_stats
tools.description: Aggregate statistics for the prompt registry: counts by repo, status, task type, grade, average score, top tags, and recent capture rate. Use to understand the prompt database composition.

tools.name: prompt_tag
tools.description: Add or remove tags on a stored prompt. Tags are validated against the docs taxonomy (mcp, agents, go, terminal, etc). Use to organize and categorize prompts for easier retrieval.

tools.name: ps_list
tools.description: List running processes sorted by CPU, memory, or PID. Optionally filter by command substring.

tools.name: ps_tree
tools.description: Show process tree for a given PID using pstree. Falls back to ps --forest if pstree is unavailable.

tools.name: sandbox_create
tools.description: Create a new Docker sandbox container for dotfile testing. Uses the host's RTX 3080 via nvidia-container-toolkit for GPU-accelerated Hyprland headless rendering. Dotfiles are bind-mounted read-only from the host working tree.

tools.name: sandbox_destroy
tools.description: Destroy a sandbox container and clean up resources. Use force=true to remove even if running.

tools.name: sandbox_diff
tools.description: Compare sandbox symlink and config state against the host dotfiles working tree. Reports missing, modified, extra, and broken symlinks.

tools.name: sandbox_exec
tools.description: Execute an arbitrary command inside a sandbox container. Returns stdout, stderr, exit code, and duration.

tools.name: sandbox_list
tools.description: List all managed sandbox containers with status, profile, and age.

tools.name: sandbox_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the sandbox's headless Hyprland display via grim. Returns the image inline as base64 PNG. Optionally waits for visual settle before capture.

tools.name: sandbox_start
tools.description: Start a stopped sandbox container.

tools.name: sandbox_status
tools.description: Get detailed status of a sandbox including GPU utilization, memory, and CPU usage.

tools.name: sandbox_stop
tools.description: Stop a running sandbox container.

tools.name: sandbox_sync
tools.description: Sync dotfiles into a running sandbox. Runs install.sh to create symlinks and reloads Hyprland config.

tools.name: sandbox_test
tools.description: Run test suite inside a sandbox. Suites: bats (BATS tests), selftest (rice validation), symlinks (symlink health), shaders (GPU GLSL compilation), config (TOML/JSON syntax), all (everything). Returns structured pass/fail results.

tools.name: sandbox_validate
tools.description: Composed: create sandbox → sync dotfiles → run all tests → capture screenshot → destroy. Returns structured results with pass/fail and screenshot. Early aborts if tests fail.

tools.name: sandbox_visual_diff
tools.description: Compare a sandbox screenshot against a reference image using ImageMagick. Returns the diff image inline and a similarity percentage.

tools.name: screen_annotate
tools.description: Open a screenshot file in swappy for annotation. The swappy GUI opens as a fire-and-forget process. Not useful if swappy is not installed.

tools.name: screen_color_pick
tools.description: Pick a color from the screen. Uses hyprpicker if available, otherwise captures a 1x1 screenshot at cursor position via wayshot. Returns the hex color value.

tools.name: screen_ocr
tools.description: Take a screenshot and extract text via OCR using tesseract. Returns the extracted text.

tools.name: screen_record_start
tools.description: Start recording the screen using wf-recorder (Wayland). Runs in the background. Use screen_record_stop to finish.

tools.name: screen_record_status
tools.description: Return the current recording state: active/inactive, PID, output path, and elapsed duration. No input required.

tools.name: screen_record_stop
tools.description: Stop an active screen recording started by screen_record_start. Returns the output file path.

tools.name: screen_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current screen or a region. Uses wayshot (Wayland). Returns the image inline and the saved file path.

tools.name: screen_screenshot_annotated
tools.description: Take a screenshot and immediately open it in swappy for annotation. Composed: wayshot capture then swappy launch.

tools.name: session_accessibility_tree
tools.description: Read the AT-SPI accessibility tree for one application inside a tracked session.

tools.name: session_click_element
tools.description: Invoke the default clickable AT-SPI action for a semantic element inside a tracked session.

tools.name: session_clipboard_get
tools.description: Read the clipboard for a tracked session using wl-paste.

tools.name: session_clipboard_set
tools.description: Write text into a tracked session clipboard using wl-copy.

tools.name: session_connect
tools.description: Create or rehydrate a session handle for the current live Wayland desktop.

tools.name: session_dbus_call
tools.description: Run a session-scoped dbus-send call for advanced KWin, portal, or app-specific automation.

tools.name: session_find_ui_element
tools.description: Find a semantic UI element inside a tracked session by app plus name, role, states, ref, or path.

tools.name: session_find_ui_elements
tools.description: Find all semantic UI elements inside a tracked session that match the app plus name, role, states, ref, or path query.

tools.name: session_focus_element
tools.description: Focus a semantic element inside a tracked session using AT-SPI component focus or a focus action fallback.

tools.name: session_focus_window
tools.description: Focus a window inside a tracked session by Hyprland address/class/title or by semantic AT-SPI window targeting for non-Hyprland sessions.

tools.name: session_invoke_action
tools.description: Invoke a specific AT-SPI action for a semantic element inside a tracked session.

tools.name: session_launch_app
tools.description: Launch an application inside a tracked session and persist a launch log.

tools.name: session_list_windows
tools.description: List windows for a tracked session. Hyprland-backed sessions use hyprctl; other sessions fall back to AT-SPI window discovery.

tools.name: session_read_app_log
tools.description: Read the most recent launch log for a tracked session, optionally filtered by app substring.

tools.name: session_read_value
tools.description: Read the current text or numeric value from a semantic element inside a tracked session.

tools.name: session_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot inside a tracked session using grim.

tools.name: session_set_text
tools.description: Set the text contents of an editable semantic element inside a tracked session. Pass an empty string to clear the field.

tools.name: session_set_value
tools.description: Set the numeric value of a semantic element inside a tracked session, such as a slider or spinbox.

tools.name: session_start
tools.description: Start and persist a desktop session handle. Supports a live handle or an optional KWin virtual backend.

tools.name: session_stop
tools.description: Stop a tracked desktop session. Live handles are marked stopped without killing the current compositor.

tools.name: session_type_text
tools.description: Type text inside a tracked session using wtype against the session Wayland display.

tools.name: session_wait_for_element
tools.description: Wait for a semantic element to appear or satisfy requested states inside a tracked session.

tools.name: session_wayland_info
tools.description: Read wayland-info for a tracked session.

tools.name: shader_audit_trail
tools.description: View the raw, append-only shader change history log. Returns all recorded shader changes with timestamps and sources.

tools.name: shader_build
tools.description: Run glslangValidator to preprocess and validate shaders. Test a single shader or all shaders. Returns structured results.

tools.name: shader_cycle
tools.description: Advance the active Kitty visual playlist (next/prev) via kitty-shader-playlist.sh.

tools.name: shader_diff
tools.description: Compare the last two shader changes from the history log. Shows what changed between shader switches.

tools.name: shader_get_state
tools.description: Read the current Kitty shader and theme state from kitty-shader-playlist state files.

tools.name: shader_hot_reload
tools.description: Force kitty to reload shader config via SIGUSR1.

tools.name: shader_list
tools.description: List all GLSL shaders in the source shaders directory. Optionally filter by category.

tools.name: shader_log
tools.description: View shader change history with computed durations. Shows when each shader was active and for how long.

tools.name: shader_meta
tools.description: Get full metadata for a shader from the manifest (category, cost, source, description, playlists).

tools.name: shader_playlist
tools.description: List curated shader playlists or pick a random shader from one. Omit name to list all playlists.

tools.name: shader_preview
tools.description: Preview a shader for N seconds, then automatically revert to the previous shader. Cancels any in-progress preview.

tools.name: shader_random
tools.description: Pick a random paired Kitty visual from the active playlist and apply it.

tools.name: shader_set
tools.description: Apply a CRTty shader to kitty via kitty-shader-playlist.sh.

tools.name: shader_status
tools.description: Rich status: current shader, Kitty theme, visual label, animation state, playlist position, and auto-rotate timer status.

tools.name: shader_test
tools.description: Compile-test shaders via glslangValidator. If name is provided, test that shader; otherwise test all.

tools.name: system_disk
tools.description: List disk usage per mount point (excluding tmpfs/devtmpfs). Returns total, used, available, and percent for each filesystem.

tools.name: system_gpu
tools.description: Read NVIDIA GPU stats: name, temperature, GPU/memory utilization, VRAM usage, and power draw via nvidia-smi. Returns not_available if no NVIDIA GPU is present.

tools.name: system_health_check
tools.description: Composed health dashboard: aggregates temps, GPU, memory, disk, uptime, and updates into a single report with threshold-based warnings. Returns overall OK/WARN/CRIT status with per-subsystem detail.

tools.name: system_info
tools.description: Show system information: hostname, kernel, uptime, load average, CPU count, memory, and swap.

tools.name: system_memory
tools.description: Read RAM and swap usage from /proc/meminfo. Returns total, used, available in MB with usage percentage.

tools.name: system_temps
tools.description: Read CPU, GPU, and NVMe temperatures plus fan RPMs via lm_sensors. Returns structured sensor data. Gracefully returns partial data if sensors is not installed.

tools.name: system_updates
tools.description: Check pending package updates. Returns pacman (official) and AUR (via yay) update counts. Graceful if checkupdates or yay is not installed.

tools.name: system_uptime
tools.description: Read system uptime, load averages (1/5/15 min), and last boot time. Uses /proc/uptime, /proc/loadavg, and who -b.

tools.name: systemd_disable
tools.description: Disable a systemd unit from starting on boot/login. Critical services (sshd, NetworkManager, systemd-*, dbus, polkit) require confirm: true.

tools.name: systemd_enable
tools.description: Enable a systemd unit to start on boot/login.

tools.name: systemd_failed
tools.description: List failed systemd units.

tools.name: systemd_list_timers
tools.description: List active systemd timers with their next/last trigger times.

tools.name: systemd_list_units
tools.description: List systemd units, optionally filtered by state.

tools.name: systemd_logs
tools.description: Fetch recent journal logs for a systemd unit.

tools.name: systemd_restart
tools.description: Restart a systemd unit.

tools.name: systemd_restart_verify
tools.description: Composed workflow: restart a systemd unit, poll until active (or timeout), and return previous state, new state, and recent logs. Eliminates the multi-step restart→status→logs dance.

tools.name: systemd_start
tools.description: Start a systemd unit.

tools.name: systemd_status
tools.description: Show detailed status of a systemd unit including active state, PID, memory, and CPU usage.

tools.name: systemd_stop
tools.description: Stop a systemd unit. Critical services (sshd, NetworkManager, systemd-*, dbus, polkit) require confirm: true.

tools.name: tmux_capture_pane
tools.description: Capture visible text content from a tmux pane. Default 50 lines of scrollback.

tools.name: tmux_kill_session
tools.description: Kill a tmux session by name.

tools.name: tmux_list_panes
tools.description: List panes in a session or window with index, dimensions, current command, and active status.

tools.name: tmux_list_sessions
tools.description: List all tmux sessions with window count, creation time, and attached status.

tools.name: tmux_list_windows
tools.description: List windows in a tmux session with index, name, pane count, active status, and layout.

tools.name: tmux_new_session
tools.description: Create a new detached tmux session with optional working directory and initial command.

tools.name: tmux_new_window
tools.description: Create a new window in an existing tmux session with optional name and command.

tools.name: tmux_search_panes
tools.description: Search across all panes in a session for a regex pattern. Returns matching lines with window/pane indices.

tools.name: tmux_send_keys
tools.description: Send keystrokes to a tmux pane. Keys can be literal text or tmux key names like Enter, C-c, etc.

tools.name: tmux_wait_for_text
tools.description: Poll a tmux pane until specific text appears. Returns the matching line or times out.

tools.name: tmux_workspace
tools.description: Create a multi-window, multi-pane tmux workspace from a declarative spec. Single tool replaces new_session + new_window + send_keys sequences.

tools.name: wallpaper_list
tools.description: List available wallpaper shaders (GLSL fragment shaders rendered as live animated wallpapers via shaderbg).

tools.name: wallpaper_random
tools.description: Set a random wallpaper shader. Picks a random GLSL fragment shader and launches it as a live animated wallpaper.

tools.name: wallpaper_set
tools.description: Set a specific wallpaper shader by name. Launches shaderbg to render the shader as a live animated wallpaper.
